fp_clusters.pattern: Provider-survey escape hatch admits PLANS / CHECKLISTS / TEMPLATES (RACI tables, 'Status: Planning')
examples: CSD sitter email survey checklist - Q4 2025
Sitter satisfaction survey: requesting ad-hoc/deep-dive questions
fp_clusters.fix: PROVIDER_SURVEY_RE bypasses the doc-type check entirely. Tighten by (a) blocking 'checklist' anywhere in title, (b) blocking 'request' anywhere in title (e.g. 'Survey Request'), (c) recognising '[draft]' in addition to '(draft)' inside NON_FINDINGS_TITLE_RE (currently only matches '\(draft\)'), and (d) requiring the BODY to contain a findings-vocabulary term ('summary', 'key findings', 'tl;dr', 'highlights') for the survey escape hatch to fire.

fp_clusters.pattern: FINDINGS_TITLE_RE admits 'Findings: Training owners …' where audience is the OWNER, not the provider
examples: Findings: Training owners bundle exploration study
fp_clusters.fix: Audience filter passes because 'trainer' appears once in body, but the study is about owners. Stricter audience: require provider term to appear in TITLE, not body, OR require body to mention provider term ≥2 times. Lower-priority — only 1/50 in this sample.

examples: Sitter Survey Request: Promotion & Social Media
fp_clusters.fix: Add 'survey request' / '\brequest\b:' to NON_FINDINGS_TITLE_RE. Already partially covered by (a) above.

fn_clusters.pattern: 'Acceleration #N:' prefix not in FINDINGS_TITLE_RE whitelist (research write-ups disguised as project tracking)
examples: Acceleration #3: Sitter self-promotion and sharing behaviors & Concept feedback
fn_clusters.fix: Add 'acceleration\s+#?\d+' to FINDINGS_TITLE_RE *or* lean on body-content fallback: if title has provider term + body contains 'executive summary' / 'key findings' / 'tl;dr', admit. Lower-precision but recovers this class.

fn_clusters.pattern: Body provider-term check missed by truncated haystack — provider terms exist in full body but not in title or excerpt the filter saw
examples: Write Up: Star Rating in Search
fn_clusters.fix: evaluate_eligibility() takes title + body; verify that the caller passes the FULL plain-text body (not a 500-char excerpt). At rover_confluence_dump.py:399 the call uses 'plain' (full body) — good. But at line 704 (--retag path) it passes 'excerpt', which is truncated. Round 2: change line 704 to pass the full plain body, OR re-fetch full body during retag, OR widen the haystack to include themes/problems columns (which contain sitter-relevant taxonomy).
